expectations.evidence: "yes, this is a SOLID violation — specifically the Liskov Substitution Principle"
"strengthened the preconditions ('only call when not full') and broadened the thrown exceptions beyond what the supertype promises"

expectations.text: Mentions callers would need `instanceof`/`is BoundedCache` branches
expectations.evidence: Shows a forEach example that would blow up, but does not articulate the 'callers must branch on type' smell that LSP exists to prevent

expectations.text: Suggests fixing by making put's contract explicit OR splitting interfaces per ISP
expectations.evidence: Three fixes: evict (recommended), change contract to PutResult, separate interface (RejectingStore)

expectations.text: Does NOT claim this is purely SRP or OCP
expectations.evidence: Explicitly rules out: "it is not SRP (one responsibility — caching) and not ISP"
